sourceName: Communication, Media and Cultural Industries Resume
resumeLength: One page; portfolio link only when the user has real work samples.

formattingRules.fontRecommendation: Modern but restrained typography; primary resume remains ATS-readable.
formattingRules.bulletStyle: Bullets should show audience, channel, output, and result or editorial value.

writingRules.tone: Clear, audience-aware, editorial, and commercially grounded.
writingRules.bulletFormula: Action + communication output/channel + audience/stakeholder + editorial or research method + reach, quality, or decision value
quantificationRules: Prefer concrete scope: sample size, stakeholder count, case volume, event size, model accuracy, project duration, budget, risk, or response time when supported by source facts.
When exact numbers are absent, use bounded evidence such as tool, method, deliverable, audience, or process scope; never invent metrics.
Use metrics differently by domain: technical performance for engineering, compliance/accuracy for audit, writing/research output for law and policy, service reliability for hospitality.

exampleBullets.strong: Produced weekly short-form content for student audiences, aligning story angles with campaign themes and engagement feedback.
exampleBullets.reason: Adds audience, cadence, channel, and editorial decision logic.

exampleBullets.strong: Interviewed 12 participants and synthesized recurring themes into an editorial brief for a cultural research project.
exampleBullets.reason: Shows research method and output instead of activity-only wording.

scoringCriteria.description: Conventional headings, readable layout, aligned dates, consistent spacing, and parser-friendly text.
Prominent sections and bullets match the target role and market context.
Bullets show action, method, scope, and outcome without unsupported claims.
Specific, concise, role-native language with no inflated AI phrasing.
Structure and density match the selected role, country, and CV/resume style.

promptSnippets.title: Communication, Media and Cultural Industries Resume routing rule
promptSnippets.content: When the target role matches Communication / Media / Cultural Industries, use Communication, Media and Cultural Industries Resume as the primary industry route and adopt this formula: Action + communication output/channel + audience/stakeholder + editorial or research method + reach, quality, or decision value.

promptSnippets.title: Communication, Media and Cultural Industries Resume language guardrail
promptSnippets.content: Preserve domain-native evidence for Communication / Media / Cultural Industries; do not convert the profile into a generic tech, finance, or consulting resume unless the user target explicitly requires it.

sourceReferences.label: Local 123456 CV sample corpus
sourceReferences.note: 56 newly added CV/resume samples from docs/sample-resume-knowledge; abstracted into rules only, without copying resume text.

sourceReferences.label: Existing local resume-learning corpus
sourceReferences.note: Previously extracted school resume guides, templates, sample resumes, and industry profiles.
